student_reviews.tip: It sounds like a lot but the only readings you have to do are Shakespeare, the quizzes are very simple they essentially boost your great. The midterm was supposed to be a test, but due to the war she required us to pick partners and have a recorded discussion about questions she gave us. The final is a paper, only about 1,700 words about anything we want about Shakespeare.

student_reviews.best_for: students who are passionate about music or would we interested in learning more about it
student_reviews.structure: Paper, Quizzes, Homework, Presentation, Participation/ Attendance/ Class discussions, Readings
student_reviews.tip: This course is a very enjoyable. Going into it, i did expect it to be less demanding workload wise, as readings are assigned every week with double entry notes to be taken by hand on a notebook, but grading wise it is rewarding and even people who have no knowledge of music prior will find themselves comfortable throughout the semester. If you do the required work, show investment and interest and take things seriously, you should end up with an A/A+. Try to not skip class as every absence is marked.
